title: 10 best herbal teas for puffy eyes
intro_desc: Herbal teas for puffy eyes are infusions made from various plants that can help reduce the appearance of puffy eyes. These teas typically contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can soothe the skin around the eyes and decrease swelling. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for puffy eyes include chamomile, green tea, and calendula. To use these teas, simply steep a tea bag in hot water, let it cool, and then place it on your closed eyes for 10-15 minutes. Not only can this help reduce puffiness, but it can also improve the overall appearance of the eye area, making individuals feel more confident and refreshed. Many people have reported that incorporating herbal teas into their skincare routine has helped them achieve a more youthful and well-rested appearance.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with puffy eyes because of its unique blend of antioxidants and flavonoids. The caffeine-free brew reduces puffiness by constricting blood vessels, which in turn decreases swelling. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties in white tea help to soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing redness and discoloration. This natural remedy provides a refreshing and rejuvenating solution for tired, puffy peepers.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Herbal white tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which help reduce inflammation and improve circulation to alleviate puffiness by neutralizing free radicals that can cause swelling.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal white tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and catechins, which help reduce inflammation and soothe swollen eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness.
Caffeine-free calming effects: Herbal white tea is naturally caffeine-free, allowing it to promote relaxation and calmness without stimulating the body, which can contribute to puffy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness in the eyes.
Bark: The bark contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure two teaspoons of high-quality white tea leaves.
Heat one cup of filtered water to a temperature of 160°f for four minutes.
Pour the hot water over the tea leaves and let steep for seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a large pot or teapot using a fine-mesh strainer.
Serve the herbal white tea hot, garnished with a slice of lemon.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden teas helps with puffy eyes because of its unique properties that reduce swelling and inflammation. The tea's flavonoids and tannins work to constrict blood vessels, which in turn reduces water retention and puffiness around the eyes. Additionally, the calming and soothing effects of littleleaf linden teas can help to relax tense facial muscles and reduce the appearance of dark circles under the eyes, leaving you with a refreshed and revitalized look.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Littleleaf linden tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin and flavonoids, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the skin around the eyes, alleviating puffiness.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in littleleaf linden tea, such as phenolic acids and flavonoids, neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to the delicate skin around the eyes, helping to reduce puffiness and dark circles.

Vasodilatory effects: Linden tea's vasodilatory properties help relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which reduces congestion and swelling under the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots help reduce inflammation and soothe swelling associated with puffy eyes.
Bark: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, bark helps alleviate congestion and relieves eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried tilia cordata leaves from a trusted source.
Measure out two teaspoons of dried leaves or four teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Heat one cup of filtered water in a pot to boiling point and then reduce heat.
Steep the measured herb in the hot water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and serve hot or allow to cool and store in refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with puffy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The tea's flavonoids and antioxidants work to constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid retention and puffiness. Additionally, pot marigold contains apigenin, a compound that has been shown to have a calming effect on the skin and muscles around the eyes, further reducing puffiness and promoting a more relaxed and refreshed appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce puffiness and swelling in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the delicate skin around the eyes, leading to puffiness.

Cicatrizant properties: Pot marigold tea has been traditionally used as a topical treatment for wound healing and skin sores, which may also contribute to its ability to reduce puffiness by promoting the repair of damaged skin tissues around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can soothe and calm irritated or inflamed skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried calendula flowers or two teaspoons of fresh petals from calendula officinalis.
Heat one cup of water in a tea infuser or a saucepan and bring it to a gentle boil.
Add the calendula flowers or petals to the boiling water and steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or mug and discard solids.
Serve warm or at room temperature, sweetened with honey if desired, within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright teas helps with puffy eyes because its natural anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and congestion. The herbal tea's antioxidants and flavonoids work to calm irritated skin and tissues, while its astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce water retention. As a result, eyebright teas can effectively alleviate puffiness and dark circles under the eyes, leaving the face looking refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright tea contains flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eye area, helping to alleviate puffiness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in eyebright tea, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of puffiness and dark circles.
Vasoconstrictive properties: Eyebright tea's vasoconstrictive properties can help reduce blood flow to the eyes, which in turn reduces swelling and puffiness by constricting the blood vessels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with puffy eyes.
Leaves: Contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eye tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and stems in the early morning when dew is still present on the plant.
Rinse the harvested material with clean water to remove any dirt or debris from the field.
Chop the cleaned euphrasia officinalis into small pieces to ensure even steeping during the brewing process.
Combine one teaspoon of chopped euphrasia officinalis with 8 ounces of boiling water and allow it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids and serving immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with puffy eyes because it has a natural ability to reduce inflammation and ease tension in the delicate skin around the eyes. The calming properties of lemon balm help to soothe and relax the tiny blood vessels under the eyes, reducing swelling and puffiness. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds help to combat water retention, further reducing the appearance of puffy eyes, leaving them looking smooth, bright, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Lemon balm tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress under the eyes, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles.
Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Lemon balm tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness in the delicate skin around the eyes.
Soothing and Calming Effects: The calming effects of lemon balm tea can help relax the skin and muscles around the eyes, reducing tension and puffiness caused by stress, fatigue, or allergies.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness in the eyes.
Bark: The bark of the lemon balm plant contains flavonoids that can help soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, reducing puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves for every eight ounces water.
Heat fresh filtered water in a saucepan or electric kettle to a simmer.
Pour the hot water over the measured lemon balm leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving and enjoy within an hour of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes. The herbal tea's antioxidants also help to reduce swelling and combat free radicals that can cause puffiness. Additionally, thyme teas' gentle astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing the appearance of dark circles and leaving the under-eye area looking smoother and more refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help reduce swelling and ease puffiness in the eye area by combating excess fluid retention.
Antioxidant properties: Thyme tea's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to puffy eyes, promoting healthy skin and underlying tissues around the eyes.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in thyme tea help constrict blood vessels and tighten skin tissue, reducing excess fluid buildup and puffiness in the eye area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing puffiness and swelling.
Flowers: The delicate flowers of thyme possess soothing and calming properties, which help to relax the skin and reduce inflammation around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers measuring one teaspoon per eight ounces water.
Heat filtered water in a teapot or kettle to a rolling boil for five minutes.
Pour the boiling water over the thyme herb mixture in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Steep the herbal mixture for seven to ten minutes depending on personal taste preference and desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and serve immediately after preparation time is complete.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marjoram teas helps with puffy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herbs in marjoram tea, such as thymol and borneol, work to reduce swelling and alleviate tension around the delicate eye area. As we sleep or experience stress, fluid can accumulate under our peepers, causing puffiness. Marjoram's calming effects help to dissipate this excess fluid, leaving eyes feeling refreshed and rejuvenated. By incorporating marjoram tea into your routine, you can bid farewell to puffy peepers and greet a brighter, more radiant you.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marjoram tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like thymol and cineole, which can help reduce puffiness by reducing swelling and inflammation in the delicate skin around the eyes.
Antioxidant activity: Marjoram tea's antioxidant properties help combat free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to puffy eyes, promoting healthy and firm skin under the eyes.
Vasodilatory effects: The vasodilatory compounds in marjoram tea can help relax constricted blood vessels and improve blood circulation around the eyes, reducing puffiness caused by fluid retention.
remedy_parts: Roots: Marjoram roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness.
Leaves: Marjoram leaves are rich in antioxidants that soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and dark circles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried marjoram leaves and flowers at dawn when they are fragrant and potent enough.
Rinse the leaves and flowers gently with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried marjoram or two teaspoons of fresh marjoram per 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the marjoram in the boiling water for five minutes to allow the flavors and essential oils to infuse fully.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids then sweeten with honey if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog rose teas helps with puffy eyes because its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties can effectively reduce swelling and ease tension. The antioxidants present in the tea also help to combat free radicals that can contribute to puffiness, leaving your skin looking smoother and more radiant. Additionally, dog rose has natural astringent properties that can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention, further alleviating puffy eyes and promoting a refreshed appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Dog rose tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which help to reduce puffiness by neutralizing free radicals and inflammation in the skin around the eyes.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help to reduce swelling and ease puffiness by reducing blood vessel dilation and congestion.
Astringent properties: Dog rose tea has astringent properties that can help to constrict blood vessels and shrink the appearance of puffy eyes, leaving them looking smoother and less swollen.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dog rose plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and puffiness around the eyes.
Bark: The bark of the dog rose plant contains flavonoids, which have been shown to improve circulation and reduce water retention, helping to alleviate puffy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosa canina flowers, using scissors, and wash them gently with cold water.
Combine 20-30 grams of dried flowers or 40-50 grams of fresh petals with boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a teapot.
Discard the solids and add honey to taste, stirring well to combine before serving hot.
Pour the tea into cups and allow it to cool slightly before serving it immediately or storing it in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel teas helps with puffy eyes because of its natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active ingredients, such as gallic acid and tannins, help to reduce swelling and tighten the skin around the eyes, diminishing the appearance of puffiness. Additionally, witch hazel's antiseptic properties soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort. By drinking this tea or using cooled compresses on the eyes, one can enjoy a refreshing and rejuvenating experience that leaves them looking and feeling more refreshed.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins in witch hazel tea constrict blood vessels, reducing puffiness and inflammation around the eyes by limiting fluid accumulation.
Astringents: Astringent compounds in witch hazel tea help to shrink swollen tissues and reduce redness by drying out excess moisture, making it an effective natural treatment for puffy eyes.

Antioxidants: Antioxidants present in witch hazel tea, such as gallic acid and ellagic acid, combat oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin around the eyes.
Bark: The bark has natural astringent properties that constrict blood vessels, reducing puffiness and inflammation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried witch hazel leaves and stems according to your preference and needs.
Combine one teaspoon of crushed witch hazel leaves with one quart of boiling water for a strong infusion.
Steep the mixture for 15 to 20 minutes to allow the active compounds to release into the water.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container for serving.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with puffy eyes because its calming properties help to reduce swelling and inflammation. The tea's flavonoids, such as luteolin, work to constrict blood vessels, which reduces fluid buildup and diminishes the appearance of puffiness. Additionally, the tea's soothing aroma can also help to relax facial muscles, reducing tension that can contribute to puffy eyes. By drinking English lavender tea regularly, you can say goodbye to pesky morning puffiness and hello to a more refreshed and radiant look.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and puffiness by neutralizing free radicals that can cause eye irritation.
Calming and anti-inflammatory effects: Lavender's calming compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, reduce swelling and alleviate itchiness, making it easier to manage puffy eyes.
Vasodilatory properties: Lavender tea's ability to dilate blood vessels can help reduce puffiness by increasing blood flow and reducing fluid accumulation under the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lavender roots help reduce swelling and ease puffiness.
Seeds: Containing calming and soothing properties, lavender seeds relax the skin and eye muscles, alleviating pressure and puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavender buds and leaves, measuring approximately one tablespoon per cup.
Combine harvested lavender with boiling water in a teapot or infuser, allowing steeping time of five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot, discarding solids immediately.
Add honey or sugar to taste, if desired, and stir gently to combine with warm tea.
Enjoy the soothing, calming effects of lavender tea, brewed to perfection in approximately 10 minutes total.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat puffy eyes with herbal teas is by using chamomile or green tea bags. First, steep two tea bags in hot water for a few minutes, then let them cool down. Once cooled, place the tea bags over your closed eyes for about 15-20 minutes. The anti-inflammatory properties of these teas help reduce swelling and soothe the skin around your eyes. For best results, do this daily or whenever needed.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to puffy eyes that are treated with herbal teas include eye inflammation, dark circles, and eye strain. Chamomile tea, when cooled, can reduce puffiness and soothe irritation due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Green tea, rich in antioxidants, can reduce the appearance of dark circles and improve blood circulation. For eye strain, cucumber or chamomile tea compresses can alleviate discomfort and relax the eyes. However, it is essential to consult a healthcare professional for persistent or severe symptoms.
